commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Leprosy. Do nothing which may expose you to the danger of being infected, and if you have the misfortune to contract it, obey the directions of the priests. (Calmet)
It seems from this and the following verse that God frequently punished disobedience to his ministers, as he did Mary , (Numbers xii.,) by inflicting upon them this shameful disorder. (Haydock)
So he punished king Ozias, 2 Paralipomenon xxvi. (Menochius)
The design of this precept is, therefore, not so much to order people not to contract a disease, which they cannot perhaps always avoid, as to caution them against pride and rebellion. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
